etymology.description: 大(大) (dà, "big; great") conveys size or importance, 同(同) (tóng, "same; together") suggests unity or harmony, and 區(區) (qū, "area; district") designates a geographical division.
etymology.interpretation: 大同(大同) (dàtóng, "Great Unity") originates from a Confucian ideal in the Book of Rites (禮記/礼记), describing a utopian society characterized by harmony and fairness; it refers here to a place name, conveying a sense of significant unity or community. 區(區) (qū, "area; district") simply denotes the administrative division.

examples.chinese: 大同区(大同區)有许多历史建筑(有許多歷史建築)。
examples.pinyin: Dàtóng Qū yǒu xǔduō lìshǐ jiànzhù.
examples.english: Datong District has many historical buildings.

examples.chinese: 我想去大同区(我想去大同區)的夜市(的夜市)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ xiǎng qù Dàtóng Qū de yèshì.
examples.english: I want to visit the night market in Datong District.

examples.chinese: 大同区(大同區)是台北的重要区域(是台北的重要區域)。
examples.pinyin: Dàtóng Qū shì Táiběi de zhòngyào qūyù.
examples.english: Datong District is an important area in Taipei.
